message.content: {"cases":[{"input_lines":["{\"10\": [], \"2\": [[2, 5]], \"3\": []}"],"focus":"All nodes are effectively singletons (one has only a self-loop). Tests handling of the \"all isolated\" rule and tie-breaking by smallest integer ID."},{"input_lines":["{\"1\": [[3, 5], [3, 2], [1, 10]], \"3\": [[5, 0]], \"5\": [[3, 0]], \"7\": [[8, 4]], \"8\": [[7, 4]]}"],"focus":"Parallel edges with differing weights, self-loop to ignore, zero-weight edge, and asymmetric listing (edge given only one direction) to test normalization and undirected treatment."},{"input_lines":["{\"001\": [[200, 1000000000]], \"200\": [[300, 5]], \"300\": [[200, 5]], \"400\": []}"],"focus":"Leading-zero node ID, very large weight (1e9), and a singleton component to be excluded; tests integer interpretation of IDs and large-weight handling."}]}
